explanation.summary: The value of 'total' (int) is assigned to 'small' (uint8_t). This implicit narrowing conversion may silently truncate the value (332 → 44), causing data loss and incorrect program behaviour.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows an implicit narrowing conversion from int to uint8_t.

fix_suggestions.title: Explicit cast
fix_suggestions.why: Prevent data loss by using an explicit cast.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the value of 'total' is correctly converted to 'uint8_t' without data loss.

fix_suggestions.title: Use a larger data type
fix_suggestions.why: Avoid implicit narrowing conversions by using a larger data type that can hold the original value.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Changing the data type of 'small' to 'int8_t' allows it to hold the original value of 'total' without data loss.

risk_analysis.why: The implicit narrowing conversion may cause data loss and incorrect program behaviour.
potential_failures: Incorrect program behaviour due to data loss

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Implicit narrowing conversions are not allowed according to MISRA2012-RULE-10_3.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that the fix does not introduce new issues or complications.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.
Rejected 'Use a temporary variable': Patch declares new variable(s) not present in original snippet. Cannot verify these are safe to introduce.

results.guideline_title: The standard input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used

explanation.summary: The standard input/output library <stdio.h> is being used, which is a violation of MISRA2012-RULE-21_6.
explanation.rule_basis: This code violates MISRA2012-RULE-21_6 because the standard input/output library <stdio.h> shall not be used. Restricted standard-library features are disallowed because they reduce determinism, analyzability, or portability.
explanation.code_evidence: The use of printf function in line 30.

fix_suggestions.title: Replace printf with a safer alternative
fix_suggestions.why: The printf function is not compliant with MISRA2012-RULE-21_6. Replacing it with a safer alternative will ensure compliance.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Using the standard library's iostream instead of printf ensures compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-21_6.
fix_suggestions.risk_reduction: Using a safer alternative reduces the risk of non-compliance and potential runtime errors.

fix_suggestions.title: Use a logging library
fix_suggestions.why: Using a logging library will ensure that the standard input/output library is not used directly and provide additional logging capabilities.
fix_suggestions.patched_code: // Add a logging library and use it instead of printf
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Using a logging library ensures compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-21_6.
fix_suggestions.risk_reduction: Using a logging library reduces the risk of non-compliance and potential runtime errors.

risk_analysis.why: The use of the standard input/output library <stdio.h> can lead to non-compliance and potential runtime errors.
potential_failures: Runtime errors due to the use of the standard input/output library

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions to ensure compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-21_6.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Non-compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-21_6 can lead to potential runtime errors and reduced portability.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all instances of the standard input/output library <stdio.h> are replaced with compliant alternatives.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear inconsistent. The warning text suggests a violation of Rule 21.6, but the retrieved context also mentions Rule 21.7. It is unclear if the code violates both rules or just Rule 21.6.
Rejected 'Use a custom function to print the value': Patch declares new variable(s) not present in original snippet. Cannot verify these are safe to introduce.
